name: my-skills
description: Stage-scoped plugins for agentic .NET development: workflow-core (ops/meta + pipeline orchestrators), system-definition (research/spec/plan), architecture (ARCH/C4/ADRs + .NET/Azure scaffolding), and development (Aspire/EF Core/Agent Framework references + build/verify). Enable per stage to keep skill context minimal.

plugins.name: workflow-core
plugins.description: Cross-cutting ops/meta + pipeline orchestrators: bootstrap & manage workflow.json + .claude/settings.json, declare external plugin marketplaces, validate config/secrets, and the end-to-end build / research-only conductors. Drives stage switching; keep enabled across stages.

plugins.name: system-definition
plugins.description: Stage 1 — decide WHAT to build: industry research -> product spec (SPEC.md) -> plan + RBAC (PLAN.md). Enable at project kickoff; disable once the plan is frozen so its descriptions stop costing context.

plugins.name: architecture
plugins.description: Stage 2 — decide the SHAPE once: ARCH.md + C4 + ADRs (cloud-agnostic), plus the .NET/Azure solution skeleton + Terraform + GitHub Actions scaffolding. Enable to define the initial architecture, then disable for the long development phase.

plugins.name: development
plugins.description: Stage 3 — continuous .NET 10 development. Aspire, EF Core 10, and Microsoft Agent Framework references that Claude reaches for automatically while coding, plus the Aspire solution backbone, pluggable-connector pattern, build orchestrator, and runtime verify loop. The default coding-stage plugin.
